note: Well-typed Compat_m across profile spaces: one shared occurrence, two analyses; the target sets are never intersected directly (formal audit R2 item 11).

instance.boundary: the chess game as played; each engine acts only by evaluating and proposing moves

loss.reason: the compatibility illustration ranges over win-sets only; no loss functional is exercised by it

tolerance.reason: placement is over discrete win/loss/draw profiles; no numeric tolerance applies

merger_family.reason: a single perfect-information observation channel supplies the placement; nothing is merged

instance.governance_boundary: the engine operator; no external governance authority is declared for this illustration

instance.boundary: the chess game as played; each engine acts only by evaluating and proposing moves

loss.reason: the compatibility illustration ranges over win-sets only; no loss functional is exercised by it

tolerance.reason: placement is over discrete win/loss/draw profiles; no numeric tolerance applies

merger_family.reason: a single perfect-information observation channel supplies the placement; nothing is merged

instance.governance_boundary: the engine operator; no external governance authority is declared for this illustration

compatibility_assessment.definition: exists m' in Reach(m) with O*(m'; A_white) in G_white AND O*(m'; A_black) in G_black
compatibility_assessment.reason: zero-sum win-sets: no reachable terminal realizes a win-profile under BOTH analyses; draw successors realize neither (they are not counterexamples to Conflict)
compatibility_assessment.note: the two placements 'white-advantage' and 'black-disadvantage' are CONSISTENT: one fact under two declared analyses, not a contradiction
